skill.name: GitHub Project & Contributor Finder API Skill
skill.description: Find GitHub repository project details and contributor contact information using keywords, stars, and update date filters. Makes API calls to BrowserAct service to retrieve contributor data including profiles, social links, and repository summaries.
skill.summary: Extract GitHub project details and contributor contact information via API
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Risk factors confirmed: network access to external API, environment variable for API credentials. External commands are benign shell invocations for running Python scripts. No malicious intent detected. Code is legitimate API client for GitHub project/contributor data retrieval service.

high_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Credentials
high_findings.description: Script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables. This is standard practice for API key management and is appropriate for this skill. The key is used solely for authenticating to the BrowserAct service.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key access is intentional for authentication; credentials are not exfiltrated or stored improperly

low_findings.title: External Command Execution for Python Script Invocation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents shell commands to run Python scripts. These are benign invocations for legitimate API client execution, not arbitrary command injection. No user input concatenation into shell commands detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shell commands use hardcoded paths and arguments; script parameters are passed as function arguments, not shell-expanded user input

low_findings.title: Network Access to External API Service
low_findings.description: Python script makes HTTP requests to BrowserAct API endpoint. This is the intended purpose of the skill - retrieving GitHub project and contributor data via a third-party service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are to legitimate BrowserAct API for data retrieval; API endpoint is documented in skill instructions

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs for Documentation Links
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains hardcoded URLs for BrowserAct documentation and pricing pages. These are informational links for user guidance, not dangerous network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to legitimate BrowserAct service documentation; no data exfiltration or malicious destinations

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Error Messages
low_findings.description: Script checks for specific error strings in API responses. This is standard error handling and diagnostics, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: String matching is for API error detection; no system information gathering or network probing

content.user_title: Find GitHub Contributors and Project Details
content.value_statement: Finding qualified developers and project details on GitHub manually requires extensive searching. This skill searches repositories by keywords, stars, and dates to automatically extract contributor profiles and contact information.
actual_capabilities: Search GitHub repositories by keywords with minimum star count filtering
Filter repositories by last update date to find recently active projects
Extract contributor profiles including username, bio, and social links
Retrieve personal website and Twitter handle from GitHub profiles
Get repository summaries and owner information for discovered contributors
Paginate through multiple pages of search results
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for operation
Data extraction depends on BrowserAct service availability and rate limits
GitHub profile completeness varies; some fields may be empty for users without bios or social links

use_cases.title: Tech Recruiter Building Candidate Pipeline
use_cases.description: Recruiters search for developers who contribute to popular projects in specific domains like machine learning or web frameworks. Extract contact information to reach out with job opportunities.
use_cases.target_user: Technical recruiters and talent acquisition teams

use_cases.title: Developer Outreach for Tool Marketing
use_cases.description: Marketing teams find active contributors to repositories related to their developer tools product. Collect email addresses and social profiles for targeted campaigns.

use_cases.title: Open Source Project Discovery
use_cases.description: Developers and researchers find trending repositories in their area of interest by filtering on keywords and star counts. Discover projects to contribute to or learn from.

prompt_templates.title: Find Popular Repositories by Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Find GitHub repositories related to {topic} with at least {min_stars} stars that were updated after {date}
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to discover popular projects in a specific technical domain

prompt_templates.title: Get Contributor Contact Information
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for GitHub repositories about {topic} and extract contact information for the top contributors including their personal websites and Twitter accounts
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to reach out to active contributors in a specific area

prompt_templates.title: Build Lead List for Developer Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Find repositories related to {tech_stack} that have been updated recently. For each repository, get the top 3 contributors with their profile URLs and bios
prompt_templates.scenario: When building a lead list for developer tool promotion

prompt_templates.title: Research Active Contributors in Domain
prompt_templates.prompt: Search GitHub for {domain} projects with {min_stars}+ stars updated after {date}. Get detailed profiles including their other repositories, personal websites, and social media
prompt_templates.scenario: For comprehensive research on active developers in a field

output_examples.input: Find GitHub repositories about browser automation with at least 500 stars updated after 2025-01-01
output: Repository: Puppeteer (URL: github.com/puppeteer/puppeteer, 85000 stars, Owner: Google)
Contributors: [{name: 'Jane Smith', bio: 'Developer at Google', website: 'janesmith.dev', twitter: '@janesmith'}],
Repository: Playwright (URL: github.com/microsoft/playwright, 78000 stars, Owner: Microsoft)

output_examples.input: Search for machine learning repositories with 1000+ stars and extract contributor profiles
output: Top contributors: [Alex Chen (TensorFlow), Maria Garcia (PyTorch), James Wilson (scikit-learn)]
Contact extraction complete. 15 contributor profiles retrieved including bio, website, and Twitter.

output_examples.input: Find React component libraries with 2000+ stars and get owner contact info
output: Discovered popular React libraries: Material-UI, Chakra UI, Ant Design
Owner profiles retrieved with social links for partnership outreach

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before using this skill
Use specific keywords and higher star counts to get more relevant and high-quality repository results
Check the status logs during execution; the task may take several minutes depending on pagination settings
anti_patterns: Do not use overly broad keywords without star filters; this returns too many low-quality results
Do not set extremely high pagination counts on first run; start with page_turns=1 to verify results
Do not ignore API key errors; an invalid or expired key will prevent any data retrieval

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Visit https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations to create an account and generate your API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script will output an 'Invalid authorization' error. Do not retry; instead provide a valid API key or check if your subscription is active.

faq.question: How long does the search take?
faq.answer: Execution time depends on the number of pages and items per page. A single page with 5 items typically takes 1-3 minutes. Monitor status logs for progress.

faq.question: Can I search without specifying minimum stars?
faq.answer: Yes, the stars parameter defaults to 100 if not specified. However, using higher star counts helps filter for more established and valuable repositories.

faq.question: What data is extracted for each contributor?
faq.answer: The skill extracts: GitHub username, profile URL, bio, summary of their other repositories, personal website link, and Twitter handle if available.

faq.question: Is there a limit on how many results I can retrieve?
faq.answer: The limit depends on your BrowserAct subscription plan. Higher pagination and date limits per page will process more results but may hit rate limits or concurrent task restrictions.
